conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I do have children I'm responsible for. I'm asking about my two kids - Alex who's 9 and Blake who's 5. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're asking about Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5), both living with you at home.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to ask you some questions:

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, are you the main person who looks after them and is responsible for their day-to-day care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. I'm the one who looks after them day-to-day. They live with me and I'm responsible for their care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask: Are Alex and Blake both under 16 years old? (I know you mentioned Alex is 9 and Blake is 5, I just want to confirm their current ages are correct.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct. Alex is 9 and Blake is 5, so they're both under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Next question: Are either Alex or Blake in any form of education or training? (For example, are they in school, college, or any approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both in school. Alex is in primary school, year 5, and Blake is in year 1.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Now I need to ask: Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £50,000 per year? This is important because it may affect whether you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit Charge.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not go into my income details right now - I'm mainly just trying to understand if I'm actually eligible for Child Benefit in the first place. Does my income affect whether I can claim it at all?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I understand. To answer your question: **No, your income does not affect whether you are eligible to claim Child Benefit.** You can claim Child Benefit regardless of your income level.

However, if you or your partner earn over £50,000, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the High Income Child Benefit Charge when you complete your tax return. But this doesn't stop you from being eligible to claim it.

Let me ask a few more questions to complete the eligibility assessment:

Are Alex and Blake both usually living with you in the UK (apart from temporary absences like holidays or school trips)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me here in Manchester. They're with me all the time apart from the odd school trip or if they visit their grandparents for a weekend, that sort of thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just a couple more questions: Are Alex and Blake your own children, or are they related to you in another way (for example, are they adopted, step-children, or children you're fostering)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're my own children, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One more important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake? (For example, the children's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information.

Just one final question to complete my assessment: Are there any other circumstances I should be aware of? For example:
- Are Alex or Blake subject to immigration control or visa restrictions?
- Do you have the right to reside in the UK (are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or another immigration status)?
- Are either of the children in care or looked after by the local authority?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. We're UK citizens, the kids aren't in care or anything - they're just living at home with me like any normal family. Everything's straightforward.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

**Here's why:**

**For Alex (age 9):**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are the person responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- All eligibility criteria are met ✓

**For Blake (age 5):**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You are the person responsible for Blake's day-to-day care
- Blake lives with you in the UK
- You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- All eligibility criteria are met ✓

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. As a reminder, while income doesn't affect your eligibility to claim, if you or a partner earn over £50,000 a year, you may need to pay back some or all of the benefit through the High Income Child Benefit Charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
